title: acer glabrum subsp. diffusum
intro: Acer glabrum subsp. diffusum, also known as the Rocky Mountain maple, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Acer glabrum subsp. diffusum include its ability to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, from minor cuts and scrapes to more serious conditions such as eczema and bronchitis.
